metadata.description: Parallel plate (lamella) settler design parameters from AguaClara water treatment design library
metadata.reference: Weber-Shirk et al., AguaClara: Open-Source Water Treatment Plant Design
metadata.notes: Parameters adapted for industrial wastewater primary clarification

upflow_velocity_mm_s.description: Design upflow velocity through plate stack
upflow_velocity_mm_s.notes: Lower for high TSS (>500 mg/L), higher for low TSS applications

capture_velocity_mm_s.description: Minimum particle settling velocity to be captured
capture_velocity_mm_s.notes: Based on target particle size removal. 0.12 mm/s ≈ 50 µm particles

hydraulic_loading_multiplier.description: Surface loading rate multiplier compared to conventional clarifiers
hydraulic_loading_multiplier.notes: Lamella clarifiers can handle 3-5× higher loading than conventional

plate_angle_degrees.description: Angle of lamella plates from horizontal
plate_angle_degrees.notes: 60° is standard. Steeper angles (55-65°) better for sludge sliding

plate_spacing_cm.description: Perpendicular distance between adjacent plates (WASTEWATER DEFAULT)
plate_spacing_cm.drinking_water_standard: 2.5 cm (AguaClara default for low-TSS potable water)
plate_spacing_cm.industrial_adaptation: Increase to 5.0 cm for very high TSS (>800 mg/L) or fibrous solids

plate_length_m.notes: Longer plates = higher removal but more expensive. Limited by structural support.

manifold_orifice_diameter_mm.notes: Larger orifices for high TSS to prevent clogging

applications: Retrofit of existing clarifiers (add plates to increase capacity)
Industrial wastewater with settleable solids (TSS 200-2000 mg/L)
Primary clarification ahead of biological treatment
advantages: Higher hydraulic loading rates (120-200 m³/m²/d vs. 30-50 for conventional)
Smaller footprint (60-70% reduction in plan area)
Shorter HRT (0.5-1.0 hour vs. 1.5-2.5 for conventional)
Modular design (easy to expand or retrofit)
Good performance with variable flow rates
limitations: Requires consistent influent TSS (wide fluctuations cause problems)
More susceptible to clogging than conventional (need good screening)
Not suitable for very high TSS (>2000 mg/L) without pre-treatment
Sludge removal must be frequent to prevent re-suspension

industrial_adaptations.high_tss: Increase plate spacing to 3-4 cm (vs. 2.5 cm standard)
industrial_adaptations.oil_grease: Add coalescing media or pre-treatment DAF unit
industrial_adaptations.temperature_effects: Adjust upflow velocity: ±0.1 mm/s per 10°C from 20°C
industrial_adaptations.chemical_dosing: Coagulation can improve removal by 15-25% (optimize with jar tests)

number_of_plates.description: Number of plates that fit in given height

total_settling_area.description: Total projected settling area for all plates
total_settling_area.notes: This is the effective area for SOR calculations

hydraulic_loading_rate.description: Surface overflow rate based on total plate area

tss_removal.notes: Performance similar to conventional clarifiers but at higher loading rates

particle_size_cutoff.description: Particles larger than this are typically removed (>90% efficiency)
particle_size_cutoff.notes: Based on capture velocity of 0.12 mm/s

capex_multiplier.description: CAPEX compared to conventional clarifier (same capacity)
capex_multiplier.notes: Lower due to smaller footprint and less excavation

opex_considerations: Lower pumping energy (shorter distances, less head loss)

outputs.notes: Wider spacing reduces clogging risk with high TSS
